elementsAdapted: Monospace text-only interface with minimal chrome
Dense, information-rich layout with no decorative elements
Numbered navigation items for keyboard-first access
Left-aligned text stream as primary content display
inspirationSources.whatChanged: Applied mining/geological theme instead of tech news. Used amber-on-dark-slate palette instead of gray-on-white. Added ASCII art banner. Replaced link-list with terminal command-response pattern.

elementsAdapted: Full-screen terminal application paradigm
Function key shortcuts displayed in a bottom/top bar
Text-based file tree with expand/collapse
Status bar at bottom showing system state
inspirationSources.whatChanged: Used number keys instead of function keys. Removed dual-pane file manager layout in favor of single-stream terminal output. Applied mine shaft content persona. Modern web implementation instead of ncurses.

elementsAdapted: Compact stat grid with progress bars
Real-time clock in status area
Color-coded status indicators
Dense grid layout for metrics
inspirationSources.whatChanged: Replaced CPU/memory metrics with project management KPIs. Used amber/green color coding instead of htop's rainbow palette. Simplified bar charts to single-color fills. Added mine-themed labels.

elementsAdapted: Keyboard-driven navigation with visible shortcut indicators
Command echo showing what action was taken
Activity log with timestamps
Bordered panels for different data sections
inspirationSources.whatChanged: Replaced git operations with idea management operations. Used single-panel stream instead of multi-panel split. Applied geological/mining terminology. Commands are navigational rather than operational.

elementsAdapted: Green/amber phosphor text on dark background aesthetic
Field-based input forms with prompt markers
Status line at bottom of screen
No mouse interaction assumed (keyboard-first)
Fixed-width character grid alignment
inspirationSources.whatChanged: Used warm amber instead of classic green. Added mouse/touch support alongside keyboard for accessibility and mobile. Modern CSS grid instead of fixed character positions. Richer typography mixing monospace and decorative headings.

antiRepeatCompliance.pass1_carved_monolith: No recessed left rail, no etched panels, no carved inset shadows. This pass is flat terminal with zero depth.
antiRepeatCompliance.pass2_layered_strata: No protruding top tablets, no stone tabs, no depth layers. This pass has no elevation concept.
antiRepeatCompliance.pass3_obsidian_forge: No obsidian glass, no volcanic glass, no razor edges, no ember glow. This pass uses matte amber on flat dark slate.
antiRepeatCompliance.pass4_sandstone_canyon: No sandstone canyon, no desert sunset palette. This pass uses dark mine interior with amber terminal colors.
antiRepeatCompliance.pass5_marble_temple: No marble, no polished columns, no classical proportions, no gold-leaf. This pass is raw terminal aesthetics.
antiRepeatCompliance.pass6_concrete_bunker: No military bunker, no reinforced steel, no tactical overlays. This pass uses mining terminal theme.
avoidedPatterns: recessed left rail, engraved items, etched panels
protruding top tablets, stone tabs, depth layers, geological header
obsidian glass, volcanic glass, razor edges, ember glow
marble temple, polished columns, classical proportions, gold-leaf
aegis shield, military bunker, reinforced steel, tactical
accordion cavern, collapsible sidebar, split-panel detail, crystal formations

uniquenessNotes: This is the only pass across all Slate variants that uses a fullscreen terminal paradigm with keyboard shortcut navigation displayed as numbered key badges. No other pass uses ASCII art as a hero element, terminal command-response as content flow, or a mine foreman persona. The interaction model (typewriter cascade, blinking cursor, spark flash, [OK] micro-feedback) is wholly distinct from all other passes.
